title: The 9 Best Herbal Creams to Fight Phlegm Buildup

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for phlegm because it has natural expectorant properties that help to break down and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways.
Quercetin is good for phlegm because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Tannins are good for phlegm because they have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to reduce mucus production and clear out excess phlegm from the lungs and airways.
Eucalyptol is good for phlegm because it helps loosen and clear out excess mucus and phlegm from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
Polyphenols are good for phlegm because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion and mucus production in the airways, making it easier to breathe and clear out phlegm.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus cream is a popular remedy for phlegm problems. It works by loosening thick mucus in the throat and chest, making it easier to cough up. This is because eucalyptus has natural decongestant properties that help reduce swelling in the airways. When the airways are less swollen, it's easier for air to flow in and out, which can help to break up phlegm. The cream is often applied to the chest or throat to help ease congestion and make breathing easier. Some people also find it helpful to inhale the vapors of eucalyptus globulus oil, which can help to thin out phlegm and make it less sticky. By loosening phlegm and reducing congestion, eucalyptus globulus cream can help to relieve symptoms of phlegm and make it easier to breathe.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hyptis suaveolens herbal creams that help with phlegm, first you'll need to extract the oil from the plant. Start by drying the leaves of the Hyptis suaveolens plant, either by air-drying or using a dehydrator. Once the leaves are completely dry, you can use a solvent like coconut oil or olive oil to extract the oil from the leaves. To do this, simply place the dried leaves in a glass jar and cover them with the solvent. Let it sit for a few weeks, shaking the jar regularly, until the solvent has absorbed the oil from the leaves. Next, filter the mixture to separate the oil from the solvent and discard the solids. Now, you can mix the extracted oil with a base cream, like shea butter or coconut oil, and other natural ingredients like beeswax and essential oils. Finally, heat the mixture gently to melt the beeswax and mix everything together. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool, and your Hyptis suaveolens herbal cream is ready to use.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for phlegm

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for phlegm because it has antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory system, making it easier to expel excess mucus and phlegm.
Carvacrol is good for phlegm because it has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help to break down and clear out phlegm, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
Rosmarinic acid is good for phlegm because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce mucus production and ease congestion in the respiratory system.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris cream, also known as thyme cream, is a natural remedy that can help with phlegm issues. Phlegm is a thick, yellowish or white mucus that forms when our body tries to protect itself from infections. Thyme has antibacterial properties that help fight off the germs causing the infection. When applied topically as a cream, thyme can help break down excess phlegm and reduce its thickness. This makes it easier to cough up or expel the phlegm, providing relief from congestion. Additionally, thyme has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and chest. This can make breathing and swallowing more comfortable. Some people also find that thyme cream helps open up airways, allowing for smoother breathing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hyptis suaveolens herbal cream that helps with phlegm, you will need dried Hyptis suaveolens leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, and essential oil. Start by infusing the dried leaves in a carrier oil like coconut oil for several weeks, allowing the goodness of the plant to seep into the oil. Once the infusion is ready, strain the oil and discard the leaves. In a double boiler, melt the beeswax and shea butter together, then add the infused oil and stir until well combined. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly before pouring it into a container. Add a few drops of essential oil, like peppermint or eucalyptus, which are known for their decongestant properties, to give it a pleasant scent and extra benefits. Finally, let the mixture cool and solidify completely before applying it to the chest or nose to help relieve congestion and phlegm.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for phlegm

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for phlegm because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe and expel excess mucus.

Oleanolic acid is good for phlegm because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the respiratory system, making it easier to breathe and clear out excess mucus.
Caryophyllene oxide is good for phlegm because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion and ease coughs by loosening and clearing excess mucus from the airways.

Carnosic acid is good for phlegm because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and congestion in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and clear out excess mucus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis cream, also known as rosemary cream, is good for phlegm because it has natural ingredients that help to loosen and clear out mucus from the body. The main component of rosemary, a chemical called carnosic acid, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the nose and sinuses, making it easier to breathe. This helps to clear out phlegm and mucus that can cause congestion and coughing. The cream also has antiseptic properties that prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can make respiratory problems worse. Additionally, rosemary has a decongestant effect, which means it helps to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing air to flow more freely. When applied topically, the cream can also help to soothe and calm the skin and mucous membranes, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by phlegm and mucus.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hyptis suaveolens herbal creams that help with phlegm, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Hyptis suaveolens leaves, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, vitamin E oil, and a double boiler. Begin by infusing the dried Hyptis suaveolens leaves in coconut oil for a few hours or overnight, allowing the oil to absorb the herb's properties. Strain the oil and discard the leaves. In a separate bowl, melt the shea butter and beeswax together in the microwave or in a double boiler. Combine the infused oil with the melted shea butter and beeswax mixture, and stir well. Add a few drops of vitamin E oil to help preserve the cream and enhance its benefits. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Hyptis suaveolens herbal cream is ready to use. Apply it to the chest or nose to help relieve phlegm and congestion, and enjoy the natural benefits of this herbal remedy.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for phlegm

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for phlegm because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the respiratory tract.
Saponins are good for phlegm because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help break down excess mucus and phlegm in the respiratory system, making it easier to cough out and breathe.
Alkylamides help to reduce phlegm because they have anti-inflammatory properties that calm the respiratory tract and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe and expel excess mucus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams are used to help with phlegm. This is because Echinacea purpurea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the body, making it easier to breathe when you have a cold or flu. Phlegm is a thick, sticky liquid that comes out when you cough. When you have too much phlegm, it can be hard to breathe. Echinacea purpurea creams can help to loosen the phlegm and make it easier to cough it out. This can help to clear your airways and make it easier to breathe. The creams can also help to soothe an irritated throat and reduce the amount of phlegm that you produce. This can be especially helpful when you have a cough that is causing you to cough up a lot of phlegm.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hyptis suaveolens herbal cream that helps with phlegm, you will need some fresh or dried Hyptis suaveolens leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few other simple ingredients. First, you need to dry or dry-fry the Hyptis suaveolens leaves to make them ready for use. This can be done by spreading the leaves on a paper or a cloth in a well-ventilated area for a few days or by drying them in a low-temperature oven. Once the leaves are dry, you can make a herbal infused oil by mixing them with your carrier oil in a clean glass jar and leaving it in the sun for a few weeks, shaking the jar every day. After the infusion is ready, you can filter it through a cheesecloth and discard the solids. To make the cream, you will need to mix the infused oil with melted beeswax in a double boiler, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens. Finally, you can add some other ingredients like vitamin E oil or essential oils to enhance the cream's benefits and pour it into a clean glass jar for storage.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for phlegm

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for phlegm because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the chest and lungs, making it easier to breathe.
Menthol is good for phlegm because it helps to thin and loosen phlegm, making it easier to cough up and clear out the airways.
Catechins are good for phlegm because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and clear out excess mucus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a natural herb that has been used for centuries to relieve congestion and coughs caused by excess phlegm. The active ingredient in peppermint, menthol, helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to cough up. When applied topically as a cream, menthol in peppermint can ease congestion in the chest and sinuses by opening up airways and reducing inflammation. This helps to clear out phlegm and mucus, giving you relief from stuffy noses and coughs. Additionally, peppermint cream can also help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by excess phlegm. When used as directed, peppermint cream can be a safe and effective way to manage symptoms of excess phlegm and promote a sense of clearness and ease in the chest and sinuses.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hyptis suaveolens herbal cream that helps with phlegm, you'll need dried Hyptis suaveolens leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few other simple ingredients. First, combine 1/4 cup of dried Hyptis suaveolens leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil in a saucepan and gently heat it over low heat for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally, to make a herbal infusion. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, and discard the solids. Next, melt 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler or in the microwave in 10-second increments, being careful not to overheat it. Once the beeswax is melted, slowly pour it into the herbal infusion, stirring constantly to prevent separation. Add a few drops of vitamin E oil and a tablespoon of aloe vera gel to the mixture to enhance its moisturizing properties and shelf life. Pour the mixture into small glass jars and let it cool and solidify before using it as a cream to help relieve phlegm and congestion.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for phlegm

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin helps to reduce phlegm by acting as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up.

Flavonoids are good for phlegm because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and fight off infections in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and expel mucus.
Liquiritigenin is good for phlegm because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and block the production of mucus in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is a natural remedy that helps to reduce phlegm. The main ingredient, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the throat and chest area. This makes it easier to breathe and feel more comfortable when you have a cough or cold. The cream also helps to break down mucus, making it easier to expel from the body. When used regularly, it can reduce the thickness and amount of phlegm produced, making it a helpful tool in managing chronic coughs and respiratory issues. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infections. This makes Glycyrrhiza glabra cream a popular choice for people who suffer from phlegm and respiratory problems.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Hyptis suaveolens herbal cream that can help with phlegm, start by gathering dried Hyptis suaveolens leaves, which are also known as sweet leaf or bushy mint. First, prepare a herbal infusion by steeping 1-2 teaspoons of dried Hyptis suaveolens leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes. Strain the mixture and discard the solids. Next, combine the infused liquid with 1/2 cup of coconut oil or a carrier oil of your choice in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil has melted and the mixture has cooled slightly. Remove from heat and let it cool completely. Once cooled, add 1/4 teaspoon of beeswax and 1/4 teaspoon of vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir until the beeswax has melted, then pour the mixture into a clean glass jar. Allow it to set at room temperature until it has thickened into a smooth, creamy texture. Your Hyptis suaveolens herbal cream is now ready to use as needed, applying it to your chest or nose to help loosen and clear phlegm.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for phlegm

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for phlegm because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce congestion and mucus production in the body, making it easier to breathe and clear out phlegm.
Shogaols are good for phlegm because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the production of mucus and ease congestion in the respiratory system, making it easier to breathe.
Zingiberene is good for phlegm because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce congestion and clear out mucus.
Polyphenols are good for phlegm because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and mucus production in the body, making it easier to expel excess phlegm.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been used for centuries to help with congestion and coughs. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the chest and throat. When applied topically in a cream form, these compounds can be absorbed into the skin and provide relief from phlegm buildup. Ginger creams can also help thin out mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear out the airways. The warming sensation of ginger can also help to loosen and clear out phlegm, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues. Additionally, ginger has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that can contribute to infections and further exacerbate phlegm buildup.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hyptis suaveolens herbal creams that help with phlegm, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Hyptis suaveolens leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, and a carrier oil like olive or jojoba oil. Begin by making a tea from the dried leaves by steeping them in hot water for about 10-15 minutes. Let the tea cool down, then strain it and mix it with the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler. Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax is fully melted, then remove it from the heat and let it cool slightly. Next, add the carrier oil and mix well to combine. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely before use. Once the cream is set, you can apply it topically to the chest or throat to help relieve congestion and phlegm. Note that you should do a patch test before using the cream to ensure you're not allergic to any of the ingredients.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for phlegm

Quercetin is good for phlegm because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the body, making it easier to expel excess mucus.
Ursolic acid is good for phlegm because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and lungs, making it easier to breathe and clear out excess mucus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis is a type of herb also known as sage. The cream made from this herb has been used for centuries to help with coughs and congestion. The active ingredients in sage cream, such as thujone and camphor, have natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to break down and clear out thick mucus in the airways, making it easier to breathe. When applied topically to the chest or nose, the cream can also help to reduce swelling and ease congestion. This makes it a popular natural remedy for relieving symptoms of a cold or flu. Additionally, sage cream has antiseptic properties that can help to prevent infections in the respiratory tract.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hyptis suaveolens herbal cream that helps with phlegm, you will need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, harvest the Hyptis suaveolens leaves and flowers, and dry them completely in a cool, dark place. Once they are dry, grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, combine 2 tablespoons of the powder with 8 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat. Stir the mixture until the powder is fully dissolved in the oil, then remove it from the heat. Let it cool and solidify completely. After it has solidified, add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the mixture. Melt the beeswax and shea butter in the microwave or over low heat, then stir them into the Hyptis suaveolens mixture until it is fully combined. Pour the mixture into a small container and let it cool and solidify completely. Once it has cooled, your Hyptis suaveolens herbal cream is ready to use. Apply it to the chest or nose to help relieve phlegm and congestion.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for phlegm

Tannins are good for phlegm because they have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling in the throat and lungs, making it easier to breathe and clear out mucus.
Roserinic acid is good for phlegm because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the body, allowing for easier drainage of excess mucus.
Kaempferol is good for phlegm because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughs and colds.
Luteolin is good for phlegm because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and clear out excess mucus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hyptis suaveolens cream is a natural remedy that can help with phlegm issues. It contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the throat and nasal passages. This makes it easier to breathe and swallow. The cream also has expectorant properties, which means it helps to loosen and clear out thick phlegm. This can be especially helpful for people with conditions like bronchitis or pneumonia. Additionally, Hyptis suaveolens has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off infections that can cause phlegm buildup. When applied topically, the cream can also help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing discomfort and coughing. Some people use it to relieve congestion and sinus pressure, while others use it to help manage postnasal drip. It is often recommended for its natural and gentle approach to addressing phlegm issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hyptis suaveolens herbal cream for phlegm relief, start by harvesting fresh leaves and flowers of the plant, also known as Sweet Marjoram. Clean and dry the harvested parts thoroughly to remove any dirt or moisture. Then, chop the dried leaves and flowers into fine pieces and mix them with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a 1:3 ratio. Leave the mixture to infuse in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it occasionally, to allow the essential oils to be extracted. After the infusion is complete, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container. Next, mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax and shea butter to achieve a creamy texture. Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax and shea butter are fully melted, then let it cool and solidify. Once solidified, your Hyptis suaveolens herbal cream is ready to use. Apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day to help reduce phlegm and relieve congestion.
remedies.image_alt: Hyptis suaveolens herbal creams for phlegm

intro_desc: If you have a lot of phlegm in your body, it can be uncomfortable and even painful. Phlegm is a thick liquid that your body produces to protect itself from infection, but too much of it can clog your airways and make it hard to breathe. Herbal creams can help to clear out the excess phlegm and ease your symptoms. One of the key herbs used in these creams is Eucalyptus globulus, also known as eucalyptus oil. This oil has natural decongestant properties that help to break down and clear out mucus and phlegm from your airways. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease congestion. Another important herb used in these creams is Thymus vulgaris, or thyme. Thyme has antibacterial properties that can help to fight off the underlying infection that's causing your body to produce excess phlegm. By using herbal creams that contain these herbs, you can start to feel better and breathe more easily. Fixing this issue is important because when you have too much phlegm in your body, it can lead to a range of problems, including coughing, wheezing, and even pneumonia. If left untreated, these problems can be serious and even life-threatening. By clearing out the excess phlegm, you can improve your quality of life and be able to do the things you enjoy without feeling tired and uncomfortable. You'll be able to breathe easily, sleep better, and have more energy to tackle your daily tasks. This can be especially important for people who have conditions like bronchitis or asthma, as it can help to manage their symptoms and prevent flare-ups. By using herbal creams that contain eucalyptus oil and thyme, you can start to feel better and take control of your health.
supplementary_best: To remedy phlegm, a combination of Eucalyptus globulus, Zingiber officinale, and Glycyrrhiza glabra is effective. Eucalyptus globulus contains eucalyptol, which is a natural expectorant that helps to break down and eliminate phlegm from the lungs. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease congestion in the airways. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, has demulcent properties that can soothe and protect the mucous membranes, making it easier to expel phlegm. This combination of herbs works well together because the expectorant properties of Eucalyptus globulus help to loosen and clear phlegm, while the anti-inflammatory properties of Zingiber officinale reduce swelling and ease congestion, and the demulcent properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra soothe and protect the mucous membranes, making it easier to get rid of phlegm.
supplementary_avoid: If you have phlegm, it's best to avoid herbal creams that may make it worse. One such cream contains 'Hydrastis canadensis', which is also known as goldenseal. This herb can dry out your mucous membranes, causing your body to produce more phlegm. Another herb to avoid is 'Aloe barbadensis', also known as aloe vera. Aloe vera can cause an allergic reaction in some people and make their phlegm thicker. Also, 'Foeniculum vulgare', or fennel, can be a problem because its seeds can irritate your throat and make your phlegm worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: One natural remedy that can help with phlegm is salt water gargling. When you gargle with salt water, it helps loosen and clear out the mucus in your throat. This is because salt has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and kill bacteria, which can cause phlegm buildup. Gargling also helps to thin out the mucus, making it easier to expel from your body.  Another remedy that can be helpful is steam inhalation. When you inhale steam from a bowl of hot water or a steam humidifier, it helps to loosen and clear out the mucus in your lungs and airways. The heat from the steam helps to break down the mucus, making it easier to cough up. This can be especially helpful when you have a cold or flu, as it can help to clear out the mucus that's causing congestion.  Drinking warm liquids, such as tea or broth, can also be helpful in thinning out mucus and making it easier to cough up. The warmth of the liquid helps to loosen the mucus, making it easier to expel from your body. This can be especially helpful when you're feeling congested and need to clear out your airways.  In addition to these remedies, some people find that drinking apple cider vinegar can help to thin out mucus and reduce congestion. The acidity in the vinegar helps to break down the mucus, making it easier to cough up. However, it's worth noting that this remedy may not be suitable for everyone, especially those with certain health conditions or who are taking certain medications.  Honey has also been found to be helpful in reducing congestion and coughing. When you mix honey with warm liquids, such as tea or broth, it helps to soothe the throat and reduce inflammation. The antibacterial properties of honey also help to kill bacteria that can cause phlegm buildup.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have phlegm often experience other ailments like sinusitis, bronchitis, and coughs. These conditions are caused by an overproduction of mucus, which is a thick and sticky substance that can clog the airways and make it hard to breathe. Medicinal herbs like thyme, eucalyptus, and peppermint can help to reduce phlegm and alleviate these symptoms. Thyme has antibacterial properties that can fight off infections and reduce inflammation in the respiratory system. Eucalyptus has decongestant properties that can help to break up mucus and make it easier to expel, while peppermint can help to ease congestion and coughs by opening up the airways. Additionally, people with phlegm may also experience fatigue, headaches, and a sore throat, which can be relieved by herbs like ginger, which has anti-inflammatory properties, and slippery elm, which can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes. These herbs can help to reduce the production of phlegm and alleviate the symptoms associated with these conditions.
